OPPOSITION SUPPLEMENTAL TO DEFTS MOTION FOR ATTY'S FEES FILED BY PLAINTIFF KATO, YOKO ("ZIGGIE")

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 1916-26 MASON STREET HOA'S MOTION TO DETERMINE ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS TO BE AWARDED TO PREVAILING PARTY IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $20,000 AS REASONABLE FEES AND $721.66 IN COSTS. DEFENDANT'S SUBMISSION - WHICH REDACTS THE HOURLY RATES CHARGED TO ITS CLIENT AND REDACTS SIGNIFICANT PORTIONS OF ITS BILLING RECORDS - MAKES IT HARD FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE THE AMOUNT OF REASONABLE FEES TO BE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT PER THE FEES CLAUSE IN THE CC&RS. IN THE ABSENCE OF ANY EVIDENCE OF THE HOURLY RATES ACTUALLY CHARGED BY ITS COUNSEL AND THE EVIDENCE SUGGESTING THAT ITS COUNSEL WAS BILLING AT INSURANCE DEFENSE RATES, THE COURT BELIEVES THAT $200 PER HOUR IS AN APPROPRIATE HOURLY RATE. A REVIEW OF THE DEFENDATN'S BILLING RECORDS AND THE NATURE OF THE WORK DONE REVEALS THAT APPROXIMATELY HUNDRED HOURS OF ATTORNEY WORK IS APPROPRIATE TO BE COMPENSATED PER THE FEES CLAUSE. THE CLAIMED FEES APPEAR TO BE EXCESSIVE. DEFENDANT CAN ONLY RECOVER FOR FEES INCURRED ON THE CLAIMS RELATING TO INTERPRETING OR ENFORCING THE CC&RS AND SHOULD NOT RECOVER FOR COMMUNICATIONS WITH THE INSURER. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT CONTEST ANY OF THE COSTS SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077 =302/HK

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Determine Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Be Awarded To Prevailing Party
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLAIM R. CARLSON OF PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, TEL. NO. 278-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT 1916-26 MASON STREET HOA's Motion To Determine Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Be Awarded To Prevailing Party is Granted in part. Defendant is awarded $20,000 as reasonable fees and $721.66 in costs. Defendant's submission - which redacts the hourly rates charged to its client and redacts significant portions of its billing records - makes it hard for the court to determine the amount of reasonable fees to be awarded to defendant per the fees clause in the CC&Rs. In the absence of any evidence of the hourly rates actually charged by its counsel and the evidence suggesting that its counsel was billing at insurance defense rates, the court believes that $200 per hour is an appropriate hourly rate. A review of the defendatn's billing records and the nature of the work done reveals that approximately hundred hours of attorney work is appropriate to be compensated per the fees clause. The claimed fees appear to be excessive. Defendant can only recover for fees incurred on the claims relating to interpreting or enforcing the CC&Rs and should not recover for communications with the insurer. Plaintiffs do not contest any of the costs sought by defendant. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: La Vena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion To Determine Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Be Awarded To Prevailing Party

WILLIAM CARLSON, ESQ., PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, 415.278.9400, FOR DEFENDANT 1916-26 MASON STREET HOA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT 1916-26 MASON STREET HOA's Motion To Determine Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Be Awarded To Prevailing Party is continued to October 3, 2012. Defendant shall file and serve copies of its contemporaneous billing statements with appropriate redactions, if any, by September 19, 2012. Plaintiff may file and serve a supplemental opposition commenting on the billing statements by September 26, 2012. All parties are to provide courtesy copies to department 302 per Local Rule 2.6B. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
